title: Bit Flip
description:
## Bit Flip

Apply an **X gate** (Pauli-X / quantum NOT) to qubit 0 so that the qubit starts in |0⟩ and ends in |1⟩.

### Goal
Your circuit should produce the statevector:
```
|1⟩  →  [0, 1]
```

### Background
The X gate is the quantum equivalent of a classical NOT gate. It flips |0⟩ → |1⟩ and |1⟩ → |0⟩.

| Input | Output |
|-------|--------|
| \|0⟩   | \|1⟩   |
| \|1⟩   | \|0⟩   |

### Constraints
- Use exactly **1 qubit**
- No measurement gates in your solution circuit

### Classical Equivalent
A NOT gate: `bit = 1 - bit`
hints: You only need a single gate on qubit 0.
The X gate (also called the Pauli-X gate) flips the qubit state.
In the Circuit Composer, drag the X gate onto qubit 0 at step 0.
